#set( $h2 = '##' )
# ${rootArtifactId}

> `${groupId}:${rootArtifactId}:${version}` — Java 25 and Spring Boot 4.1 multi-module DDD application with explicit authenticated caller and tenant context, MyBatis-Plus, MySQL, Flyway, optimistic locking, after-commit domain events, and optional Redis.

$h2 Primary documentation

- [Chinese project overview and quick start](README.md): 简体中文快速开始、架构、安全与测试说明。
- [English project overview](README.en.md): requirements, commands, module summary, security model, and test entry points.
- [Architecture and invariants](docs/architecture.md): dependency direction, request flow, transactions, tenancy, persistence, cache, and events.
- [HTTP API reference](docs/api-reference.md): routes, authorization, query constraints, response shapes, status values, and errors.
- [Development workflow](docs/usage-guide.md): how to add commands, queries, aggregate behavior, repository operations, migrations, HTTP endpoints, and errors.
- [Configuration and security](docs/configuration.md): profiles, environment variables, trusted-header restrictions, Flyway, Redis, MyBatis, and observability.
- [Object layering and naming](docs/object-layering.md): Request, Response, AuthenticatedCaller, VO, aggregate, value object, PO, converter, error, and naming rules.
- [Testing guide](docs/test-guide.md): unit, adapter, security, transaction, migration, and MySQL Testcontainers tests.
- [Agent instructions](AGENTS.md): mandatory rules and verification workflow for automated contributors.

$h2 Architecture summary

- Runtime adapters depend on application; application depends on domain.
- Domain does not depend on API, application, shared, or infrastructure modules.
- Every use case receives `AuthenticatedCaller` and derives an explicit `TenantId`.
- Repository and cache operations require tenant and fail closed.
- Commands use `CommandServiceTemplate`; reads use `QueryServiceTemplate`.
- Application operation/error-scene identifiers use `UseCaseOperation`; domain facts use `*Event`.
- Flyway is the only schema initializer.
- Aggregate `version` provides optimistic concurrency control.
- `UserStatus.DELETED` is the only soft-delete representation.
- Cache and event side effects execute after transaction commit.
- Redis is optional and cannot change business correctness.
- Trusted identity headers are disabled by default and restricted to dev/test profiles.

$h2 Code landmarks

Search these class names with `rg` because generated Java paths include the chosen package:

- `Bootstrap`: application composition and entry point.
- `UserController`: REST endpoints under `/api/v1/users`.
- `SecurityConfig`: route authorization and trusted-header profile guard.
- `AuthenticatedCallerMapper`: verified Spring Security principal to API context.
- `UserServiceImpl`: explicit caller/tenant command and query orchestration.
- `CommandServiceTemplate`, `QueryServiceTemplate`, `ServiceOperation`: use-case execution policy.
- `AfterCommitExecutor`: post-commit cache and event effects.
- `User`: aggregate behavior, reconstruction, and persistence synchronization.
- `UserRepository`: tenant-scoped domain persistence port.
- `UserRepositoryImpl`: MyBatis-Plus adapter and optimistic locking.
- `UserPOConverter`: aggregate/PO mapping.
- `V1__create_user_table.sql`: canonical initial Flyway migration.
- `NoOpCacheService`, `RedisCacheService`: optional cache adapters.

$h2 Verification

- Fast suite: `sh ./mvnw test`
- Docker/MySQL integration suite: `CI=true sh ./mvnw test`
- Full build: `sh ./mvnw clean install`

$h2 Security warning

`X-Dev-User-Id` and `X-Dev-Tenant-Id` are local development/test credentials, not a production authentication protocol. Never allow untrusted clients or a public proxy to establish identity, tenant, authorities, or administrator state through headers or request bodies.
